title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 14: Problem Solving
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Zoe counted yellow stars on her sticker sheet. How many yellow stars are there?
questions.image_description: Five stars in a row
questions.solution: Count the stars one by one: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. There are 5 stars in total.

questions.question_text: What is 4 + 3?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='15' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='35' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='55' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='75' cy='30' r='8'/><text x='95' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='18'>+</text><circle cx='120' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='140' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four circles plus three circles
questions.solution: Count all the circles together: 4 plus 3 equals 7.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly 4 equal sides and 4 right angles?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='18'/><rect x='60' y='12' width='36' height='36'/><polygon points='130,12 148,48 112,48'/><rect x='170' y='18' width='50' height='24'/><polygon points='240,12 255,30 240,48 225,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Circle, square, triangle, rectangle, and rhombus
questions.solution: A square has 4 equal sides and 4 right angles. A rectangle has right angles but sides are not all equal.

questions.question_text: Among the numbers 8, 3, 11, 5, and 7, which is the smallest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='20' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>12</text><text x='60' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>8</text><text x='100' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>15</text><text x='140' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>10</text><text x='180' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The numbers 12, 8, 15, 10, 9
questions.solution: Compare the numbers: 3 is smaller than all the others.

questions.question_text: What number comes next in the pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='10' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>2, 4, 6, 8, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Sequence of even numbers ending with a question mark
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time. After 8 comes 10.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you count in the picture?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><polygon points='20,10 35,45 5,45'/><polygon points='70,10 85,45 55,45'/><polygon points='120,10 135,45 105,45'/><polygon points='170,10 185,45 155,45'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four triangles drawn in a row
questions.solution: Count each triangle: 1, 2, 3, 4. There are 4 triangles.

questions.question_text: Lily has 9 apples. She gives 2 to her friend. How many apples does she have left?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='40' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='100' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='120' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='140' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Nine apples shown as circles
questions.solution: Start with 9 apples and take away 2: 9 minus 2 equals 7.

questions.question_text: A clock shows 3 o'clock. What does the hour hand point to?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='75' y2='50'/><text x='46' y='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>12</text><text x='80' y='54' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>3</text><text x='47' y='88' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>6</text><text x='14' y='54' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Analog clock showing 3 o'clock
questions.solution: At 3 o'clock, the hour hand points directly to the number 3.

questions.question_text: What number should replace the question mark so that 7 + ? = 13?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='20' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>7 + ? = 13</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Equation seven plus question mark equals thirteen
questions.solution: Since 13 minus 7 equals 6, the missing number is 6.

questions.question_text: Tom has two coins of 5 cents and three coins of 2 cents. How much money does he have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='19' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>5c</text><circle cx='65' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='59' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>5c</text><circle cx='130' cy='30' r='12'/><text x='124' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='9'>2c</text><circle cx='165' cy='30' r='12'/><text x='159' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='9'>2c</text><circle cx='200' cy='30' r='12'/><text x='194' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='9'>2c</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two 5-cent coins and three 2-cent coins
questions.solution: Two 5-cent coins make 10 cents. Three 2-cent coins make 6 cents. Together 10 plus 6 equals 16 cents.

questions.question_text: In the pattern circle, square, circle, square, circle, ?, what shape comes next?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='12'/><rect x='48' y='18' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='100' cy='30' r='12'/><rect x='128' y='18' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='12'/><text x='215' y='36' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='22'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Alternating circles and squares ending with a question mark
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle, square, circle, square. After the fifth circle, a square comes next.

questions.question_text: Anna is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Cara. Who is the shortest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='10' width='30' height='60'/><text x='28' y='78' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Anna</text><rect x='80' y='25' width='30' height='45'/><text x='90' y='78' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Ben</text><rect x='140' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><text x='148' y='78' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Cara</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars showing Anna tallest, Ben middle, Cara shortest
questions.solution: Anna is taller than Ben, and Ben is taller than Cara. So the order from tallest to shortest is Anna, Ben, Cara. Cara is the shortest.

questions.question_text: How many squares are in the 3 by 2 grid below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='20' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='60' width='40' height='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A grid with 3 columns and 2 rows of small squares
questions.solution: There are 3 columns times 2 rows equals 6 small squares in the grid.

questions.question_text: A box contains 12 pencils. If 5 pencils are taken out, how many remain?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='20' width='100' height='50'/><text x='40' y='50' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='14'>12 pencils</text><line x1='130' y1='45' x2='170' y2='45'/><polygon points='170,45 160,40 160,50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A box labeled 12 pencils with an arrow out
questions.solution: Starting with 12 pencils and taking 5 away gives 12 minus 5 equals 7 pencils left.

questions.question_text: How many sides does a heptagon have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><polygon points='50,10 85,30 85,70 50,90 15,70 15,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A regular hexagon
questions.solution: A heptagon is a 7-sided polygon.

questions.question_text: Mia read 8 pages on Monday and 6 pages on Tuesday. How many more pages did she read on Monday than on Tuesday?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='10' width='80' height='20'/><text x='30' y='25' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='12'>Mon: 8</text><rect x='20' y='40' width='60' height='20'/><text x='30' y='55' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='12'>Tue: 6</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Bar chart comparing Monday and Tuesday pages
questions.solution: The difference is 8 minus 6, which equals 2 pages more on Monday.

questions.question_text: Three children share 15 stickers equally. How many stickers does each child get?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='18'/><text x='23' y='44' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='12'>?</text><circle cx='120' cy='40' r='18'/><text x='113' y='44' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='12'>?</text><circle cx='210' cy='40' r='18'/><text x='203' y='44' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='12'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three children each with a question mark showing shared stickers
questions.solution: Fifteen stickers divided equally among three children is 15 divided by 3, which equals 5 stickers each.

questions.question_text: Look at the figure. How many small triangles together make up the large triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 110' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><polygon points='60,10 110,100 10,100'/><line x1='35' y1='55' x2='85' y2='55'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='100'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Large triangle divided into four small triangles
questions.solution: The large triangle is divided into 4 small triangles of equal size.

questions.question_text: Sam has 5 red balls, 4 blue balls, and 6 green balls. How many balls does he have altogether?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='40' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='100' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='130' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='170' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='190' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='215' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='233' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='251' cy='30' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Groups of red, blue, and green balls
questions.solution: Adding all together: 5 plus 4 plus 6 equals 15 balls.

questions.question_text: In the sequence 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, ?, what number comes next?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><text x='10' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='20'>1, 3, 5, 7, 9, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Sequence of odd numbers
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time. After 9, the next odd number is 11.

questions.question_text: A frog jumps 2 steps forward and then 1 step back each time. After 3 full moves, how many steps forward from the start is the frog?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='230' y2='40'/><circle cx='10' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='70' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='100' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='130' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='160' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='190' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='220' cy='40' r='4'/><path d='M10 40 Q25 15 40 40' fill='none'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A number line with a frog jump shown
questions.solution: Each move gains 2 minus 1, or 1 step net. After 3 moves the frog is 3 steps ahead.

questions.question_text: A candy costs 4 cents. How much do 5 candies cost?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='14'/><text x='19' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>4c</text><circle cx='75' cy='30' r='14'/><text x='69' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>4c</text><circle cx='125' cy='30' r='14'/><text x='119' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>4c</text><circle cx='175' cy='30' r='14'/><text x='169' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>4c</text><circle cx='225' cy='30' r='14'/><text x='219' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>4c</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five candies each marked 4c
questions.solution: Five candies at 4 cents each: 4 plus 4 plus 4 plus 4 plus 4 equals 20 cents.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 2 o'clock and ends at 4 o'clock. How many hours long is the movie?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 110' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><circle cx='50' cy='55' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='55' x2='50' y2='25'/><line x1='50' y1='55' x2='72' y2='67'/><text x='46' y='22' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>12</text><text x='84' y='59' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>3</text><circle cx='160' cy='55' r='40'/><line x1='160' y1='55' x2='160' y2='25'/><line x1='160' y1='55' x2='182' y2='43'/><text x='156' y='22' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>12</text><text x='194' y='59' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>3</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two clocks showing 2 o'clock and 4 o'clock
questions.solution: From 2 o'clock to 4 o'clock there are 2 hours.

questions.question_text: Put 3 different digits from 1, 2, 3 into the empty boxes to make the largest three-digit number. What is that number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='15' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='80' y='15' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='140' y='15' width='40' height='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three empty boxes to fill with digits
questions.solution: To make the largest number, put the biggest digit in the hundreds place, then next, then smallest. So 3 then 2 then 1 gives 321.
